items.headline: Nine new homes approved for Old Trace Road subdivision
items.summary: City Council approved a plan to subdivide a 1-acre lot into nine single-family homes plus seven junior accessory dwelling units on Old Trace Road. This represents a significant increase in residential density for the Residential Estate zone.

items.headline: Summerhill Redevelopment Project Advances at El Camino Real
items.summary: Tree replacement work is underway for the Summerhill redevelopment at 4345 El Camino Real, a significant housing project in Palo Alto. The permit shows active progress on what appears to be a major mixed-use development that will transform a key commercial corridor.

items.headline: City Council approves lease bonds for new public safety building
items.summary: Mountain View is financing a new public safety building through lease revenue bonds, marking a significant capital project for the city. This infrastructure investment will affect emergency services facilities and city operations.

items.headline: City reviews Biodiversity and Urban Forest Plan
items.summary: The City Council held a study session on April 28 to review a draft Biodiversity and Urban Forest Plan. This plan will shape how Mountain View manages its trees, green spaces, and wildlife habitat going forward.

items.headline: Mixed-use development with 67 homes and retail proposed in Sunnyvale
items.summary: A 4.22-acre project would add 55 townhomes and 12 apartments along with 18,535 square feet of retail space, including a new two-story building while keeping the existing Speedy's Tacos location. The Planning Commission held a study session on April 27 to review the proposal before formal consideration.

items.headline: Campbell updates business license tax rates
items.summary: The City Council considered revising business license tax rates in the municipal code as part of an annual update. This affects local businesses operating in Campbell and may impact business costs.

items.headline: FIFA World Cup 2026: Large promotional signs approved near Levi's Stadium
items.summary: Santa Clara authorized temporary supergraphic signs on building surfaces around Levi's Stadium during the 2026 FIFA World Cup events. The signs will be permitted for a limited time period surrounding the special events.

items.headline: New firefighters contract approved through 2029
items.summary: The City Council approved a new three-year Memorandum of Understanding with the Santa Clara Firefighters union (Local 1171) covering the period through 2029. The contract details were not specified in the agenda summary.
